metadata.summary: Integrating design tokens into Tailwind CSS by mapping token values to CSS custom properties and referencing them in `tailwind.config.js` — using RGB channel syntax for color tokens to preserve opacity modifier functionality (`bg-primary/50`), and extending (not replacing) the default theme.

content.title: Tailwind + Design Tokens
content.summary: Integrating design tokens into Tailwind CSS by mapping token values to CSS custom properties and referencing them in `tailwind.config.js` — using RGB channel syntax for color tokens to preserve opacity modifier functionality (`bg-primary/50`), and extending (not replacing) the default theme.

why.p: Hardcoded Tailwind utility values (`text-[#3B82F6]`, `p-[13px]`) scatter design decisions and break theming. A token-backed config where every color and spacing utility references a CSS variable enables dark mode, brand variants, and white-label theming with a single token file change.

list.item: Bootstrapping a new Tailwind project that must support theming (dark mode, brand variants, white-label)
Migrating an existing Tailwind codebase from hardcoded utility values to CSS-variable-backed tokens
Generating `tailwind.config.js` theme overrides from a Style Dictionary token build output
Auditing a Tailwind codebase for arbitrary values (`text-[#...]`, `p-[...]`) that should become tokens
Setting up a Storybook token documentation layer alongside a Tailwind component library
Projects not using Tailwind — use Style Dictionary + CSS custom properties directly
Pure CSS/SCSS codebases where Tailwind migration is not planned
When design tokens are not yet defined or stable — generating config before taxonomy is finalized creates throwaway work
Utility-class-only projects with zero theming requirements — CSS variable indirection adds complexity without payoff

section.name: rgb-channel-pattern
section.p: Color tokens must use RGB channel values in CSS custom properties to enable Tailwind opacity modifiers.

rule.statement: Define color tokens as RGB channel values (not hex) in CSS custom properties to preserve Tailwind opacity modifier functionality (bg-primary/50).
rule.rationale: Tailwind opacity modifiers generate classes like bg-primary/50 by injecting an alpha channel. This only works when the color is expressed as separate RGB channels that can be combined with a transparency value. Raw hex breaks the modifier syntax entirely.

example.title: RGB channel variables for opacity support
example.note: RGB channel variable enables opacity modifiers; hex variable available for non-Tailwind consumers.

example.title: Tailwind config with alpha-value placeholder
example.note: alpha-value placeholder is Tailwind v3+ syntax for opacity modifier injection.

antipattern.description: Color token defined as hex in Tailwind config: primary: 'var(--color-primary)'
antipattern.reason: bg-primary/50 silently renders as fully opaque because the opacity modifier cannot decompose a hex CSS variable into RGB channels.

section.name: theme-extension
section.p: Always extend the Tailwind theme rather than replacing it to preserve default utilities.

rule.statement: Always extend the Tailwind default theme (theme.extend) rather than replacing it — replacement removes useful defaults like font-sans, leading-*, and the full default spacing scale.
rule.rationale: Agents frequently generate config at the theme level instead of theme.extend, silently wiping defaults that downstream components depend on.

rule.statement: Extend Tailwind's spacing scale with semantic token names (spacing.md = 16px) rather than overriding the numeric scale (spacing.4 = 16px) — both can coexist.
rule.rationale: Numeric and semantic scale coexistence allows gradual migration without breaking existing components that use the default numeric scale.

section.name: css-variable-declaration
section.p: CSS custom properties must be declared at the base layer to ensure they are available globally.

rule.statement: Declare all token-mapped CSS custom properties in a @layer base { :root { } } block — never in a component CSS file.
rule.rationale: @layer base ensures custom properties are available to all Tailwind utilities without specificity conflicts and survive PurgeCSS/JIT correctly.

rule.statement: Safelist token-generated classes that are constructed dynamically (e.g., bg-${color}) to prevent JIT purge from removing them.
rule.rationale: Tailwind JIT only keeps classes it finds as literal strings in source files; dynamic class construction is invisible to the scanner and the class is silently dropped.

section.name: pipeline-discipline
section.p: Token integration requires strict CI/CD discipline to prevent manual edits degrading the system.

rule.statement: Keep the Style Dictionary build step in the CI pipeline before tailwind build runs; never hand-edit the generated tailwind-theme.js.
rule.rationale: Manual edits to generated files are overwritten on next token sync; the generated file must be treated as a build artifact, not source of truth.

rule.statement: Enforce no-arbitrary-value via eslint-plugin-tailwindcss to prevent new hardcoded values entering the codebase after token integration.
rule.rationale: Token migration is reversible without enforcement; developers under deadline will reach for arbitrary values and the token layer degrades over time.

section.name: agent-gotchas
section.p: Common mistakes agents make when generating token configurations.

rule.statement: Agents frequently generate color tokens without RGB channel support, breaking opacity modifiers — explicitly request the `<alpha-value>` pattern.
rule.rationale: This is a Tailwind-specific syntax that agents from general training sometimes get wrong.

rule.statement: Tailwind scale naming conflicts with token naming: `spacing.4 = 16px` in Tailwind default but `spacing.md = 16px` in tokens — agents produce wrong mappings without both scales as input.
rule.rationale: Provide reference tables to avoid this conflict.

rule.statement: Generated configs extend instead of replace by default but agents sometimes generate at `theme` level, wiping defaults — verify `theme.extend` placement.
rule.rationale: Validation is essential after agent-generated config.

rule.statement: Agents do not know which generated classes will be purged by JIT — require a safelist audit after generation.
rule.rationale: Dynamic class construction is invisible to Tailwind's scanner.

content.note: Best practices: Always use RGB channel pattern for color tokens, not raw hex, to preserve Tailwind's opacity modifier functionality (`bg-primary/50`). Extend the Tailwind default theme (`theme.extend`) rather than replacing it — replacing removes useful defaults like `font-sans`. Keep the Style Dictionary build step in the CI pipeline before `tailwind build` runs; config should never be hand-edited. Use `eslint-plugin-tailwindcss` with `no-arbitrary-value` rule to prevent new hardcoded values entering the codebase. Document token-to-utility-class mapping in Storybook tokens docs page — devs should not need to read `tailwind.config.js` to know which class to use. Safelist token-generated classes that are constructed dynamically (e.g., `bg-${color}`) to prevent JIT purge.

template.purpose: Tailwind config example with CSS variable references for color and spacing tokens using RGB channel pattern
Node.js script that reads tokens.json and outputs tailwind-theme.js + tokens.css with correct RGB channel handling
